task_context.description: Design and simulate a silicon pixel tracking detector system for charged particle momentum measurement through Monte Carlo simulation. This is a detector physics design task that involves creating a multi-layer silicon sensor array optimized for particle tracking while maintaining minimal material interference. The task requires testing different geometric configurations using Geant4 simulations to evaluate detector performance metrics including energy deposition patterns, hit detection efficiency, and material budget contributions across multiple particle energies.
task_context.goal: Achieve good momentum resolution for charged particles while minimizing material budget in a silicon pixel tracking detector
constraints: Total material budget < 0.1 X₀ per layer
Silicon layer thickness between 100-500 μm (Geant4 minimum: 100 μm)
Energy sweep capped at 30 GeV and 3 points maximum
Absorber should be zero or minimal (air gaps only)
Detector must be transparent to particles

observations.content: Step completed successfully: Generate silicon tracking detector geometry with 4 thin layers (300μm silicon, 2cm gaps)
Step completed successfully: Generate silicon tracking detector geometry with 6 medium layers (500μm silicon, 1.5cm gaps)
Step completed successfully: Generate silicon tracking detector geometry with 3 thick layers (800μm silicon, 3cm gaps)
Step completed successfully: Simulate muon tracks through thin layer detector at 1, 10, 30 GeV
Step completed successfully: Simulate muon tracks through medium layer detector at 1, 10, 30 GeV
Step completed successfully: Simulate muon tracks through thick layer detector at 1, 10, 30 GeV
Step completed successfully: Analyze thin layer simulation results to compute energy deposits, hit efficiency, and material budget
Step completed successfully: Analyze medium layer simulation results to compute energy deposits, hit efficiency, and material budget
Step completed successfully: Analyze thick layer simulation results to compute energy deposits, hit efficiency, and material budget
Step completed successfully: Compare all detector configurations to identify optimal design balancing momentum resolution and material budget
Step completed successfully: Generate comprehensive report with detector design recommendations, performance metrics, and physics validation

methodology_notes: Planned 11-step workflow: generate_geometry_thin_layers_4, generate_geometry_medium_layers_6, generate_geometry_thick_layers_3, simulate_thin_layers_4, simulate_medium_layers_6, simulate_thick_layers_3, analyze_thin_layers_4, analyze_medium_layers_6, analyze_thick_layers_3, compare_configurations, generate_final_report
